_meta.note: Reference file. All keys must exist here before adding to es.json / sw.json.

landing.feature_arpo_desc: Weights that adapt to your performance — every session.
landing.feature_double_prog_desc: Rep-to-weight ladder for strength-focused training blocks.
landing.feature_rest_desc: 3-tier timer calibrated per movement type — no guessing.
landing.feature_myo_desc: Activation set + 15-second cluster rests. More effective reps, less time.
landing.error_loading: Something went wrong loading your data. Tap below to retry.

onboarding.welcome_body: POWRLOG is built on peer-reviewed hypertrophy and strength research. It doesn't hand you a static spreadsheet — it watches how you perform, adjusts weights in real time, and builds a picture of your body over weeks and months.
onboarding.welcome_time_note: Takes about 2 minutes · Everything is editable in Settings later
onboarding.unit_subtitle: Choose the unit you think, train, and eat in — everything adapts.
onboarding.identity_subtitle: These two selections are required to personalise your training targets.
onboarding.identity_exp_beginner_desc: New to structured training or under 1 year lifting consistently
onboarding.identity_exp_beginner_note: Focuses on technique, motor patterns, and linear progression
onboarding.identity_exp_intermediate_note: Understands compound movements; ready for block periodisation
onboarding.identity_exp_advanced_desc: 3+ years of progressive training with a structured programme
onboarding.identity_exp_advanced_note: Requires higher volume and complexity to drive new adaptation
onboarding.physical_subtitle: Optional — used to calculate your calorie and macro targets. Defaults are applied if skipped.
onboarding.physical_age_note: Metabolism slows ~1–2% per decade after 25 — age adjusts your TDEE accordingly.
onboarding.goals_subtitle: What are you training for? This shapes your nutrition targets and program structure.
onboarding.goals_lose_fat_desc: Cut body fat while preserving muscle
onboarding.goals_recomp_desc: Build muscle and lose fat simultaneously
onboarding.goals_build_muscle_desc: Maximise hypertrophy and add lean mass
onboarding.goals_recomp_box_body: Best results for beginners and those returning after a long break — your body can build muscle and burn fat at the same time when you're new to training or rebuilding. Intermediate/advanced lifters typically get better results specialising in either a cut or a bulk.
onboarding.training_subtitle: These two settings shape how your program progresses. Both can be changed anytime in Settings.
onboarding.training_activity_lightly: Lightly Active — a few walks per week
onboarding.training_activity_very: Very Active — 5–6 intense sessions per week
onboarding.training_activity_extra: Extra Active — physical job + daily training
onboarding.training_method_arpo_desc: Weights adjust automatically based on your RIR each session. Best for hypertrophy — lets fatigue and readiness guide the load.
onboarding.training_method_double_desc: Hit the top of your rep range → next session adds weight. Simple, proven, great for strength-focused blocks.
onboarding.target_build_question: How much do you want to build up to?
onboarding.target_cut_question: Where do you want to end up?
onboarding.target_note: POWRLOG uses this to calculate your calorie deficit/surplus and tracks your progress over time.
onboarding.target_skip: Skip — set in Settings → Nutrition later
onboarding.pace_cut_title: How fast do you want to cut?
onboarding.pace_bulk_title: How fast do you want to bulk?
onboarding.summary_subtitle: Here's your profile — choose a program next.
onboarding.summary_macros_note: Full macro breakdown will appear in the Nutrition tab after setup.
onboarding.alert_missing_info_body: Please go back and select your biological sex and training experience.
onboarding.alert_setup_failed_body: Unable to save your profile. Please try again.

health.title: Connect Health Data
health.subtitle_ios: POWRLOG reads the following from Apple Health to automate your tracking. Grant all six for the full experience.
health.subtitle_android: POWRLOG reads the following from Health Connect to automate your tracking. Grant all six for the full experience.
health.steps_desc: Daily step count toward your activity goal
health.weight_desc: Auto-logs weigh-ins from your smart scale — no manual entry
health.bodyfat_desc: Tracks composition changes alongside weight over time
health.hrv_desc: The strongest signal for nervous system readiness
health.sleep_desc: Sleep quality directly determines your Recovery score
health.miss_out_title: Without this, you miss out on
health.miss_out_1: Automatic weigh-in logging from your scale
health.miss_out_3: Step tracking toward your activity goal
health.privacy_title: Your data never leaves your phone.
health.privacy_ios: POWRLOG reads from Apple Health locally. Nothing is uploaded, sold, or shared — ever.
health.privacy_android: POWRLOG reads from Health Connect locally. Nothing is uploaded, sold, or shared — ever.
health.cta_skip: Skip — I'll set this up later in Settings

dashboard.trial_last: Last free session — unlock to keep going
dashboard.recovery_sync_prompt: Tap sync to pull sleep, RHR & HRV.
dashboard.health_nudge_body: Grant access to steps, weight, HRV, and sleep for automated tracking.
dashboard.body_comp_nudge_body: Log measurements to unlock BMI, body fat %, FFMI, and lean mass trends.
dashboard.no_plan_body: Pick a training template or build your own mesocycle to get started.
dashboard.alert_skip_body: Are you sure? This will push your targets to next week.
dashboard.alert_restart_body: This will re-run the onboarding flow. Your workout history and custom routines are preserved.
dashboard.alert_no_recovery_ios: No sleep, RHR, or HRV data was found in Apple Health for today. Make sure your Apple Watch is set to track sleep and heart rate.
dashboard.alert_no_recovery_android: No sleep, RHR, or HRV data was found in Health Connect for today. Make sure your wearable is syncing sleep and heart rate.
dashboard.alert_sync_failed: No step data found in Health.

body.bmi_athlete_note: BMI is misleading for trained athletes. Your FFMI accounts for muscle mass and is the better metric for lifters. A higher BMI can simply mean more muscle, not more fat.
body.measurements_cta_hips: Add hip measurement to unlock body fat % and FFMI
body.measurements_cta_hips_note: The Navy formula for women requires waist, hips & neck
body.measurements_cta_waist: Add waist & neck to unlock body fat % and FFMI
body.measurements_cta_waist_note: Calculated using the U.S. Navy formula — no scale needed
body.no_composition_subtitle: BMI · Body fat % · FFMI · Lean mass
body.oneRM_subtitle: Estimate your one-rep max from any set

settings.bodyweight_subtitle: Used to calculate bodyweight exercise targets
settings.change_plan_subtitle: Browse templates and start a new mesocycle
settings.gym_type_subtitle: Switches exercises for all remaining sessions
settings.gym_home_note: Home gym mode uses dumbbells and bodyweight exercises.
settings.gym_full_note: Full gym mode uses barbells, cables, and machines.
settings.training_goal_subtitle: Changes rep targets and volume ranges from the next session
settings.progression_subtitle: How your weights and reps advance each week
settings.progression_arpo_desc: Weights adjust automatically based on your RIR each session. Best for hypertrophy — lets fatigue and readiness guide the load.
settings.progression_double_desc: Hit the top of your rep range → next session adds weight. Simple, proven, great for strength-focused blocks.
settings.custom_exercises_subtitle: Add exercises not in the library
settings.watch_subtitle: Tap Start Workout to also start a Strength Training workout on your Apple Watch.
settings.streak_subtitle: Log a workout or weigh-in every day to keep your streak alive.
settings.steps_subtitle: Synced from Apple Health or Health Connect, or entered manually on the dashboard.
settings.backup_body: Your workout history, weigh-ins, body measurements, custom routines, and training plan are all stored locally on your device. Export a backup to keep a copy safe — restore it anytime if you switch phones or reinstall the app.
settings.restore_modal_warning: This will replace all current data on this device. This cannot be undone.
settings.schedule_subtitle: Set which days of the week you train
settings.purchase_full_subtitle: POWRLOG unlocked — thanks for your support.
settings.privacy_note: POWRLOG does not collect, store, or sell your personal data. All training data is stored locally on your device.
settings.danger_body: All workout history, weigh-ins, body measurements, custom routines, and settings will be permanently erased. This cannot be undone.
settings.alert_change_method_body: Switching will apply from your next session. Your logged history is not affected.
settings.alert_change_goal_body: This will update your rep targets and volume ranges from the next session.
settings.alert_switch_home_body: This will swap remaining sessions in your current mesocycle to home-gym exercises. Your logged history is unchanged.
settings.alert_switch_full_body: This will swap remaining sessions in your current mesocycle to full-gym exercises. Your logged history is unchanged.
settings.alert_restart_body: This will re-run the onboarding flow. Your workout history and custom routines are preserved.
settings.alert_delete_body: All workout history, weigh-ins, body measurements, custom routines, and settings will be permanently erased.
settings.alert_final_body: Last chance — all data will be gone forever.

workout.watch_banner: Start Strength Training on your Apple Watch
workout.watch_banner_sub: Open the Workout app → Strength Training · Tap to dismiss
workout.deload_banner_sub: Reduced volume, same weight. Focus on form and recovery.
workout.myo_card_sub: Finish strong — activation set + mini-sets for maximum effective reps.
workout.feedback_weight_low: Weight below target. Increase load next set.
workout.feedback_reps_low: Reps below target. Autoregulation will lower weight next week.
workout.feedback_below: Below target. Autoregulation will adjust next week.
workout.note_exercise_placeholder: How did this feel? Anything to remember next time…
workout.tour_step1_body: Your target weight and reps are set by the algorithm. Log what you actually lift — even if it's less. The gap is data.
workout.tour_step2_body: Tap the muscle diagram to see which muscles this exercise targets and how to perform it correctly.
workout.tour_step3_body: Tap the play icon to watch a demonstration video for this exercise.
workout.tour_step4_body: After each session, ARPO reads your RIR and adjusts next week's targets. The more accurately you log, the smarter it gets.
workout.alert_leave_body: Your progress is saved and you can resume this workout later from the dashboard.
workout.alert_big_jump_body: You logged significantly more than your target weight. Did you mean to add that much?
workout.alert_weight_low_body: The weight you logged is significantly below your previous best. Double-check your entry.
workout.alert_save_failed_body: Your workout couldn't be saved. Tap 'Save Workout' to try again.
workout.tip_1: Stop one rep shy of failure — RIR 1 means you could do one more. This is your sweet spot for hypertrophy: maximum stimulus, minimum junk volume.
workout.tip_2: Rest 2–3 minutes between sets for compound lifts, 60–90 seconds for isolation work. Under-resting tanks performance and undermines progressive overload.
workout.tip_3: Progressive overload doesn't mean adding weight every session. Improving form, adding a rep, or reducing rest time all count. The log never lies.
workout.tip_4: Sleep is the most anabolic thing you can do. 7–9 hours amplifies every adaptation from this session. No supplement comes close.
workout.tip_5: The eccentric (lowering) phase drives more hypertrophy per rep than the concentric. Take 2–3 seconds on the way down — it's free gains.
workout.tip_6: The mind-muscle connection is real. Consciously squeezing the target muscle increases EMG activity and fibre recruitment. Think into the muscle, not just through the movement.
workout.tip_7: Deloads are not rest weeks — they are adaptation weeks. Volume drops, but the tissue is rebuilding. Coming back stronger is the point.
workout.tip_8: Protein synthesis peaks ~2 hours post-workout, but total daily intake matters far more than timing. Hit your daily target first.
workout.tip_9: Volume-load (sets × reps × weight) is the true measure of a session's stimulus. A 'harder' workout isn't always a better one.
workout.tip_10: Even mild dehydration (1–2%) measurably reduces strength output. Drink water before you feel thirsty.
workout.tip_11: Pairing an antagonist muscle in your rest period (e.g. curls between press sets) increases performance on the main lift and cuts total session time.
workout.tip_12: Soreness is not a reliable indicator of a good workout. DOMS reflects novelty, not stimulus. Consistent, progressive training matters more than how sore you feel.
workout.tip_13: Accumulated fatigue masks fitness. You're often stronger than you feel mid-mesocycle — the deload will reveal it.
workout.tip_14: Myo-reps: activation set to near-failure, then 15s rest, then 3–5 mini-sets. You accumulate more effective reps in less time than straight sets.
workout.tip_15: Soreness ≠ growth. You can have a maximally effective session and feel fine the next day. Log consistently and trust the numbers.
workout.tip_16: Your MEV is the minimum volume that drives progress. Your MAV is where you grow fastest. Training below MEV wastes time; above MAV risks overreaching.
workout.tip_17: Short rest myths aside, 60s rest drops performance 10–20% and reduces total weekly volume. Longer rests = more weight = more growth.
workout.tip_18: Double Progression: once you hit the top of your rep range, next session adds weight. It's a proven ratchet for strength.

summary.title: Workout Complete
summary.subtitle: Session logged. Recover well — your next targets are ready.
summary.notif_body: Great first session. The athletes who make the best progress show up consistently — want POWRLOG to remind you?

paywall.title: POWRLOG
paywall.headline:
{{count}} sessions done.
Time to unlock.
paywall.subhead:
You've seen what POWRLOG does.
Pay once. Own it. No subscription, ever.
paywall.founding_body: Most fitness apps charge $10–15/month. POWRLOG is {{price}} once for founding members — less than a single month elsewhere. Regular price is {{regular}} once the founding batch is gone.
paywall.feature_1: ARPO auto-progression — weights adjust every session
paywall.feature_2: Double Progression mode for strength-focused blocks
paywall.feature_3: Myo-rep sets — more effective reps, less time
paywall.feature_4: Smart 3-tier rest timer calibrated per movement
paywall.feature_7: Recovery Intelligence from Apple Health / Health Connect
paywall.legal_ios:
Payment charged to your Apple ID at confirmation.
Manage in Settings → [Your Name] → App Store.
paywall.alert_no_purchase_body: No previous purchase found for this Apple ID.

templates.title: Choose Routine
templates.subtitle: Pick a training block (mesocycle) — POWRLOG will set your Week 1 targets.
templates.slots_full_sub: Delete a custom routine below to create a new one.
templates.gym_modal_note: Home gym mode swaps barbell and machine exercises for dumbbell and bodyweight alternatives.
templates.delete_modal_warning: This will permanently delete the routine and all associated workout history. This cannot be undone. Your slot will be freed up for a new routine.
templates.switch_same_body: This is your current plan. Continue where you left off, or start a fresh training block from Week 1.
templates.switch_diff_body: Starting a new plan will end this training block (mesocycle). Your completed workout history is saved, but targets and progression will reset to the new template.
templates.alert_slots_full: You can save up to {{max}} custom routines. Delete one to create a new one.
templates.alert_error: Couldn't create plan. Please try again.

builder.title: Custom Routine
builder.alert_slots_full: You can save up to 3 custom routines. Delete one from the routines screen to create a new one.

meso_complete.title: Mesocycle Complete
meso_complete.subtitle: Fatigue dissipated. Tissue adapted.
meso_complete.alert_error: Couldn't start the new cycle. Please try again.
